headline: Nasdaq and Dow Jones open lower as Iran optimism wavers
summary: 10am: Nasdaq falls furthest as stocks open lower Wall Street opened lower, with the Dow Jones down 0.8%, the S&P 500 falling 0.7% and the Nasdaq slipping 0.9% in early trade. Losses on the S&P were led by consumer and tech names, with Estée Lauder dropping 7.4%, Fair Isaac down...

headline: Scott Keane, Former Databricks, Google, and Salesforce Leader, Joins Invictus Growth Partners as Operating Partner
summary: Invictus Growth Partners ("Invictus"), a leading middle-market growth equity firm with $1.3 billion in assets under management, today announced that Scott Keane has joined the firm as an Operating Partner. Keane will work directly with Invictus portfolio companies to design and scale AI-powered Revenue Operations across Sales and Marketing, leveraging his deep experience in this area from his time at Salesforce, Google, and most recently at Databricks. headline: What does Salesforce do? Who uses it & how it changed everything

headline: ServiceNow and Salesforce Fall 5%: Is the Market Mispricing Both NOW and CRM?
summary: ServiceNow (NYSE:NOW) stock and Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) stock are both sliding roughly 5% in Tuesday’s session, a simultaneous drop that puts two of enterprise software’s most prominent names in the same uncomfortable spotlight. NOW is trading around $106, while CRM has fallen to the $184 area. The selloff isn’t coming from bad earnings. Both companies reported ... headline: Software is sick again today... and it’s not just because of Claude
summary: Investing.com -- Software stocks are notably weaker again on Tuesday, and all signs point to Claude's latest release, which allows users' computers to run tasks in their browsers, as the culprit. However, according to one market watcher, six other factors could be weighing on the sector today as well.

headline: PROLIM Expands Global Salesforce, Snowflake, and Industrial AI Capabilities by Rebranding Kasmo Digital
summary: PLANO, Texas and BANGALORE, India, March 24, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- PROLIM, a global digital transformation, industrial AI, and cloud consulting company, today announced the official rebranding of Kasmo Digital to PROLIM following its recent acquisition. headline: Software Stocks Are Souring Again. Why ‘HALO’ Companies Might Not Be a Better Bet.
summary: Bloom Energy  and  Vertiv  stocks, by contrast, are up roughly 67%.  Data center infrastructure will definitely become more capital intensive as AI expands, and companies that can deliver power and engineering firms that can execute stand to benefit.  Not every power producer will become a growth stock.
